Methods: `submit_job(req: SubmitJobRequest) -> Result<SubmitJobResponse, CliError>`, `describe_job(id: &str) -> Result<JobDescription, CliError>`, `cluster_status() -> Result<ClusterStatus, CliError>`, `alloc_status() -> Result<AllocStatusResponse, CliError>`, `node_list() -> Result<NodeList, CliError>`. Request / response types are imported from `overdrive_control_plane::api` — no shadow types. `CliError` enum variants: `ConfigLoad { path, cause }`, `Transport { endpoint, cause }`, `HttpStatus { status, body: ErrorBody }`, `BodyDecode { cause }`. On HTTP 4xx / 5xx, the client deserialises the body into `ErrorBody` and returns `CliError::HttpStatus`. A test stands up a local mock server (or a real `overdrive serve` subprocess; crafter picks), confirms `client.cluster_status()` returns `Ok(ClusterStatus { ... })`; a second test with no server running confirms `client.cluster_status()` returns `Err(CliError::Transport { endpoint })` and the cause does NOT contain a raw `ECONNREFUSED` token or a raw `reqwest::Error` Debug format (asserted via `format!("{}", err)` string search). Under ~250 LoC per ADR-0014. Covers test-scenarios.md §6.5 partial (connection refused actionable error), §3.4 partial (transport error rendering).
steps.scenario_name: CLI reqwest client loads the trust triple, calls every endpoint, and maps HTTP errors + transport failures to typed CliError with actionable messages
steps.tier: Tier 3 (real reqwest + real TLS, integration-tests feature)
steps.notes: Per ADR-0014 §2, `overdrive-cli` imports from `overdrive-control-plane::api` — add `overdrive-control-plane = { path = "../overdrive-control-plane" }` to `overdrive-cli/Cargo.toml`. This creates a dependency from a `binary` crate to an `adapter-host` crate — allowed per phase-1-foundation ADR-0003 (binaries can depend on any adapter). Add `reqwest` with `rustls-tls, json` features to workspace deps if not already. Endpoint precedence (`--endpoint` flag > env > default) is clap-level; the `ApiClient::from_config` constructor accepts a precomputed endpoint string. Per ADR-0010, the client cert is attached even though Phase 1 server doesn't validate it — Phase 5 mTLS flips one switch on the server. A future phase may move this to a sibling crate `overdrive-api-client` per ADR-0014 §The CLI HTTP client — not Phase 1.

steps.name: overdrive serve and overdrive cluster init subcommands wire the trust-triple bootstrap and server startup
steps.criteria:
The clap Subcommand tree in `main.rs` gains two new commands: `Serve { #[arg(long, default_value = "127.0.0.1:7001")] bind: String, #[arg(long)] data_dir: Option<PathBuf> }` and a refactored `ClusterCommand::Init { #[arg(long)] force: bool }` (alongside existing `Upgrade`). Per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md`, the acceptance test calls the handler functions **directly** — no `overdrive` subprocess. `overdrive_cli::commands::cluster::init(InitArgs { force: false }, &clock, &fs).await` returns `Ok(InitOutput { config_path: PathBuf, endpoint: Url })`; the output names the written file and the file at `config_path` is parseable YAML matching ADR-0010 §R2. Re-invoking `cluster::init(InitArgs { force: false }, ...)` on an existing config re-mints the triple anyway (per ADR-0010 §R4 — re-init always re-mints; `--force` is reserved for future non-destructive modes) and the CA bytes differ between the two calls (byte-compare the PEM).

`overdrive_cli::commands::serve::run(ServeArgs { bind: "127.0.0.1:0".parse().unwrap(), data_dir: tmp_dir.path().into() }, &clock, &transport).await` returns `Ok(ServeHandle)` where `ServeHandle::endpoint() -> Url` carries the ephemerally-bound port and `ServeHandle::shutdown().await` triggers the cancellation path wired into `overdrive_control_plane::run_server`'s graceful-shutdown loop. The test asserts: `serve_handle.endpoint().port()` is non-zero; a reqwest probe against `serve_handle.endpoint().join("/v1/cluster/status").unwrap()` succeeds via the CLI's `ApiClient` (05-01); `serve_handle.shutdown().await` returns `Ok(())` within a 5-second deadline with any in-flight request completed; a second probe after shutdown returns `Err(CliError::Transport { ... })`. The serve handler maps a `ControlPlaneError` to an `CliError` variant when `run_server` errors out (e.g. bind failure on an occupied port). Covers test-scenarios.md §3.2 (server binds + clean shutdown), §2b.1 (cluster init writes trust triple), §2b.2 (re-init re-mints). Explicitly NOT covered here: SIGINT handling (signal delivery is a main.rs concern, not a handler concern; tested separately at the main.rs-layer in a dedicated clap-parsing test per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md` Exception clause — out of scope for Phase 1 per that same clause).
steps.scenario_name: overdrive_cli::commands::cluster::init and ::serve::run, invoked directly, mint a trust triple and bind a TLS listener on an ephemeral port; shutdown completes cleanly with in-flight-request drain
steps.tier: Tier 3 (direct CLI handler calls + real filesystem + real axum bind + real reqwest; integration-tests feature; NO subprocess per crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md)
steps.notes:
The current CLI scaffold `ClusterCommand` only has `Upgrade`. This step extends it with `Init { force: bool }`. The `Serve` subcommand is NEW to the top-level `Command` enum — it's not a cluster subcommand (mirrors Talos' `talos apiserver` shape). The `--data-dir` default is `dirs::data_dir().join("overdrive")` per XDG convention per ADR-0013 §5. For `cluster init`, the handler accepts a config-dir path via `InitArgs { config_dir: Option<PathBuf>, force: bool }` — the binary wrapper (`main.rs`) resolves `config_dir` from `$OVERDRIVE_CONFIG_DIR` env or falls back to `~/.overdrive/`; the test passes a `tempfile::TempDir` path directly to the handler (no env override needed when invoking directly). This is the benefit of the direct-call pattern per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md` — no hidden globals, no env-inheritance.

`run_server` in `overdrive_control_plane::lib.rs` returns `Result<ServerHandle, ControlPlaneError>` where `ServerHandle` carries the bound `SocketAddr` (for ephemeral-port discovery) and a `shutdown()` method. The `overdrive_cli::commands::serve::run` wraps this to return `ServeHandle` with the CLI-level endpoint/URL shape. SIGINT handling is a `main.rs`-only concern — wired via `tokio::select! { _ = tokio::signal::ctrl_c() => ..., _ = server.wait() => ... }` in the binary entry point. The acceptance test does NOT exercise SIGINT (direct-call pattern has no process signal surface); it exercises the `shutdown()` cancellation path that SIGINT ultimately drives. Integration-tests feature gated.

steps.name: overdrive cluster status and overdrive node list render the reconciler registry and honest empty states
steps.criteria: Per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md` (no-subprocess rule), the test spins up the in-process server via `overdrive_cli::commands::serve::run(...)` (from 05-02) then directly calls `overdrive_cli::commands::cluster::status(StatusArgs { endpoint }, &clock, &transport).await -> Result<ClusterStatusOutput, CliError>` and `overdrive_cli::commands::node::list(ListArgs { endpoint }, &clock, &transport).await -> Result<NodeListOutput, CliError>`. Structured output assertions (NOT stdout parsing): `cluster_status_output.mode == "single"`, `cluster_status_output.region == "local"`, `cluster_status_output.commit_index >= 0`, `cluster_status_output.reconcilers.contains(&"noop-heartbeat".to_string())`, `cluster_status_output.broker.queued/cancelled/dispatched` are typed `u64` fields. `node_list_output.rows.is_empty()` is true, `node_list_output.empty_state_message.contains("phase-1-first-workload")` is true. A separate thin rendering unit test (no server dependency) asserts `render::cluster_status(&cluster_status_output)` produces a multi-line string containing `Mode:`, `Region:`, `Commit index:`, `Reconcilers:`, `Broker counters:` labels with the corresponding values — rendering drift cannot bleed into handler correctness. Replaces the `tracing::warn!("command not yet wired to control plane")` stubs for these two commands. Covers test-scenarios.md §1.2 (cluster status WS), §6.3 (node list honest empty state), §6.4 (cluster status shows registry), §5.6 (cluster status surfaces registry).
steps.scenario_name: overdrive_cli::commands::cluster::status and ::node::list, invoked directly, return typed output carrying the reconciler registry + broker counters + commit index and an honest empty-nodes state
steps.tier: Tier 3 (direct CLI handler calls + in-process axum server + real reqwest; integration-tests feature; NO subprocess per crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md)
steps.notes: The rendering functions live in a new `crates/overdrive-cli/src/render.rs` module (extracted by 05-01's lib.rs split per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md`) so they are unit-testable without spinning up a server. Per US-05 AC, 'first output within 100ms on localhost' — the rendering path must be `println!`-based (no artificial spinners). The cluster status output format is not formally specified in the ADRs — crafter picks a readable layout (e.g. key-value pairs; mimics `talosctl cluster status`). The phrase 'phase-1-first-workload' is the canonical reference in Phase 1 per user-stories.md; the thin rendering test asserts the empty-state rendering contains it (structured-output test asserts `empty_state_message.contains("phase-1-first-workload")`). Map HTTP errors via `CliError::HttpStatus.body.message` into a user-facing error type the `render.rs` layer formats — the handler NEVER renders to stdout directly. Integration-tests feature gated for the server-backed happy-path test; the rendering unit tests land in `tests/acceptance/` under the default lane (no server).

steps.name: overdrive job submit reads TOML, constructs a Job via the same constructor, POSTs JSON, renders actionable errors on validation / connection failure
steps.criteria:
Per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md` (no-subprocess rule), the test calls `overdrive_cli::commands::job::submit(SubmitArgs { spec: payments_toml_path, endpoint }, &clock, &transport).await -> Result<SubmitOutput, CliError>` directly. Happy path: the handler reads `<toml>` via `toml::from_str` into a `JobSpecInput` (the same input shape used by `Job::from_spec`), calls `Job::from_spec` locally to validate early (fails fast before any HTTP call on malformed input), POSTs `SubmitJobRequest { spec }` via `client.submit_job`, returns `Ok(SubmitOutput { job_id: "payments".into(), intent_key: "jobs/payments".into(), commit_index: N, endpoint, next_command: "overdrive alloc status --job payments".into() })`. A separate rendering unit test asserts `render::job_submit_accepted(&output)` produces a multi-line block containing `Accepted.`, `Job ID: payments`, `Intent key: jobs/payments`, `Commit index:`, `Endpoint:`, `Next: overdrive alloc status --job payments`.

Malformed-TOML early-fail path: with `broken.toml` containing `replicas = 0`, the handler returns `Err(CliError::InvalidSpec { field: "replicas".into(), message })` — the error variant is typed, the test asserts on variant shape and that `message` contains `replicas` and `0`. NO HTTP request issued (asserted via a test-side in-process HTTP hit counter on the ServeHandle, OR by pointing the handler at an endpoint that rejects all connections and confirming no transport error surfaces — early-validation returns before the client is even constructed).

Connection-refused path: the test constructs a `SubmitArgs { spec: valid_toml_path, endpoint: Url::parse("https://127.0.0.1:1").unwrap() }` (a port nobody is listening on) and invokes the handler; the handler returns `Err(CliError::Transport { endpoint, cause })` where a structured `cause` enum variant names `ConnectionRefused` (or equivalent) and the `Display` impl on `CliError::Transport` formats three concrete next steps. Rendered form (via `render::cli_error`) contains `https://127.0.0.1:1`, explains the endpoint could not be reached, and lists three suggestions (start the control plane, verify endpoint, override endpoint); the `Debug` form of `reqwest::Error` MUST NOT appear — asserted by a `format!("{}", err).contains("reqwest")` negative check.

Replaces the `tracing::warn!` stub for `JobCommand::Submit`. Covers test-scenarios.md §6.1 (submit output carries job id + intent key + commit index + Next), §6.5 (connection refused actionable error), §6.6 (malformed spec error names field), §3.4 (CLI renders connection-refused).
steps.scenario_name: overdrive_cli::commands::job::submit, invoked directly, returns Ok(SubmitOutput) on valid input + real server; rejects malformed TOML before any HTTP call with typed CliError::InvalidSpec; connection-refused returns typed CliError::Transport whose Display form avoids raw reqwest Debug format
steps.tier: Tier 3 (direct CLI handler calls + in-process axum server + real reqwest; integration-tests feature; NO subprocess per crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md)
steps.notes: The scaffold's `JobCommand::Submit { spec: PathBuf }` uses `--spec` as a flag, but WS §1.1 and US-05 AC use POSITIONAL: `overdrive job submit payments.toml`. Change the clap definition to `JobCommand::Submit { spec: std::path::PathBuf }` with no `#[arg(long)]` — positional (one-line clap change in `main.rs`). The handler function `commands::job::submit(SubmitArgs { spec: PathBuf, endpoint: Url }, ...)` takes `PathBuf` and the resolved endpoint; the binary wrapper maps `SubmitCommand { spec }` → `commands::job::submit(SubmitArgs { spec, endpoint: config.endpoint() }, adapters)`. `toml` crate is added to `overdrive-cli/Cargo.toml`. The client-side Job::from_spec early validation is the 'fast fail' path; the server ALSO validates (defence-in-depth per ADR-0011). The client-side validation is what Phase 1 uses to name the offending field without a round-trip (AC: malformed spec error names the field — more actionable if detected locally). The 'three concrete next steps' in the connection-refused error is a static multi-line template rendered by `render::cli_error(&CliError::Transport { ... })`; no dynamic logic. The handler body never calls `println!` — handlers return typed outputs; rendering is decoupled per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md`.

steps.name: overdrive alloc status — walking-skeleton end-to-end: byte-identical spec digest via direct handler calls
steps.criteria:
**Walking-skeleton gate** — this step turns test-scenarios.md §1.1 (Ana submits a job and sees the spec digest round-trip byte-identical) GREEN, and it does so per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md` (firm rule) **by calling CLI command handlers directly as Rust functions — never by spawning the `overdrive` binary as a subprocess**. The acceptance-test entrypoint imports `overdrive_cli::commands::{cluster, serve, job, alloc}` (library surface introduced in 05-01) and invokes handlers on a shared axum-server task.

GIVEN/WHEN/THEN shape (per DWD-05 discipline) — the test translates this sequence to direct-call Rust:
```
GIVEN a fresh tempfile config directory $OVERDRIVE_CONFIG_DIR and a valid payments.toml on disk
WHEN the test calls, in sequence:
  1. overdrive_cli::commands::cluster::init(InitArgs { force: false }, &clock, &fs).await
     -- writes $OVERDRIVE_CONFIG_DIR/.overdrive/config with a freshly-minted trust triple (base64 PEM)
  2. overdrive_cli::commands::serve::run(ServeArgs { bind: "127.0.0.1:0", data_dir: tmp_dir.path().into() }, &clock, &transport).await
     -- spawns the axum-server on an in-process tokio task bound to an ephemeral port;
        the handler returns a ServeHandle carrying the actually-bound port plus a cancellation token
  3. overdrive_cli::commands::job::submit(SubmitArgs { spec: payments_toml_path, endpoint: serve_handle.endpoint() }, &clock, &transport).await
     -- parses the TOML, calls Job::from_spec, POSTs to the in-process server via the CLI's reqwest client;
        returns SubmitOutput { job_id, intent_key, commit_index }
  4. overdrive_cli::commands::alloc::status(StatusArgs { job: "payments".into(), endpoint: serve_handle.endpoint() }, &clock, &transport).await
     -- GETs /v1/jobs/payments + /v1/allocs via the same reqwest client;
        returns AllocStatusOutput { spec_digest, allocations_total, empty_state_message }
  5. serve_handle.shutdown().await -- cancels the serve task, awaits in-flight drain
THEN
  - submit_output.job_id == "payments"
  - submit_output.intent_key == "jobs/payments"
  - submit_output.commit_index >= 1
  - alloc_status_output.spec_digest == ContentHash::of(&rkyv::to_bytes::<_, 256>(&Job::from_spec(parsed_toml_input).unwrap()).unwrap()).to_string()  // byte-identical to the locally-computed digest
  - alloc_status_output.allocations_total == 0
  - alloc_status_output.empty_state_message contains "phase-1-first-workload"
  - $OVERDRIVE_CONFIG_DIR/.overdrive/config remains on disk after shutdown
```

Additionally, on `overdrive_cli::commands::alloc::status(StatusArgs { job: "mystery".into(), ... }).await` where no such job exists: the handler returns `Err(CliError::HttpStatus { status: StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, body: ErrorBody { message, ... } })`; the test asserts on variant shape + message content (names `mystery`, suggests `overdrive job submit`, per test-scenarios.md §6.7) — no stderr parsing, no exit-code check, because there is no subprocess.

Replaces the `tracing::warn!` stub for `AllocCommand::Status`. Covers test-scenarios.md §1.1 (WS-1), §6.2 (alloc status spec digest equals local compute), §6.7 (unknown job actionable error).
steps.scenario_name: Walking-skeleton end-to-end via direct CLI handler calls: cluster init → serve (in-process) → job submit → alloc status returns a spec digest byte-identical to the local rkyv compute; unknown job returns a typed CliError::HttpStatus
steps.tier: Tier 3 (walking-skeleton gate; direct CLI handler calls + in-process axum server + real rcgen + real redb + real libsql + real reqwest; integration-tests feature; NO subprocess per crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md)
steps.notes:
**This is the walking-skeleton gate.** Per DWD-01 (Strategy C — real local), every adapter in this test is real: real `rcgen` CA, real `axum` + `rustls` server, real `redb` LocalStore, real `libsql` reconciler DB, real `reqwest` client. **What is NOT real: the `overdrive` binary process.** Per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md` (firm rule), the WS test calls `overdrive_cli::commands::{cluster, serve, job, alloc}` handlers directly as Rust functions on a single test process — NO `Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_overdrive"))`, NO `assert_cmd`, NO `escargot`, NO subprocess wait-and-parse-stderr.

Library-surface requirements landing earlier in Slice 5:
- 05-01 introduces `crates/overdrive-cli/src/lib.rs` with `pub mod commands { pub mod cluster; pub mod serve; pub mod job; pub mod alloc; }`. `src/main.rs` is ≤~30 lines: install `color-eyre` + `tracing-subscriber`, parse `argv` via clap, construct real production adapters (`SystemClock`, `TcpTransport`, `reqwest::Client`), call `overdrive_cli::run(command, adapters).await`, map `eyre::Report` to exit code.
- 05-02 exposes `overdrive_cli::commands::cluster::init(InitArgs, &Clock, &FileSystem).await -> Result<InitOutput, CliError>` and `overdrive_cli::commands::serve::run(ServeArgs, &Clock, &Transport).await -> Result<ServeHandle, CliError>` — the `ServeHandle` carries the actually-bound socket address (for ephemeral-port binding: `127.0.0.1:0` → `ServeHandle::endpoint() -> Url`) and a cancellation token / shutdown method.
- 05-03 exposes `overdrive_cli::commands::cluster::status(...)`, `overdrive_cli::commands::node::list(...)` returning typed output.
- 05-04 exposes `overdrive_cli::commands::job::submit(SubmitArgs { spec: PathBuf, endpoint: Url }, &Clock, &Transport).await -> Result<SubmitOutput, CliError>`.
- 05-05 exposes `overdrive_cli::commands::alloc::status(StatusArgs { job: String, endpoint: Url }, &Clock, &Transport).await -> Result<AllocStatusOutput, CliError>` and composes all four above in the walking-skeleton test.

Adapters in the WS test: the CLI handlers accept `&impl Clock` + `&impl Transport` trait generics per DWD-05 + `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md`. In the WS test, `Clock = SystemClock` and `Transport = TcpTransport` (real adapters — DWD-01 Strategy C). A faster smoke variant of the WS under `SimClock` + `SimTransport` would run in the default nextest lane; this WS test is gated under `integration-tests` because it exercises real `rcgen`, real `redb`, real `libsql`, real `reqwest`.

The CLI-side spec-digest computation reuses the same `ContentHash::of(rkyv::to_bytes(&job))` path the server uses (03-02) — this is what makes the digests byte-identical. Do NOT implement a second canonicalisation path client-side — route through the same `overdrive-core` helpers. `CLI handlers` return structured `Output` types carrying typed fields (`commit_index: u64`, `spec_digest: String`, `empty_state_message: String`); the `main.rs` binary is responsible for rendering those outputs to stdout with a human-friendly format that contains `phase-1-first-workload` in the empty-state rendering — but the WS test asserts on the typed `Output` struct, NOT on stdout text. A separate thin rendering unit test (under `tests/acceptance/`) asserts that `render(AllocStatusOutput)` contains `phase-1-first-workload` in the empty-state string — decoupling rendering drift from handler correctness.

The scaffold `AllocCommand::Status { id: String }` uses positional `id`; the WS calls `alloc::status` with a `job` field per the GIVEN/WHEN/THEN above. Change the clap definition to `AllocCommand::Status { #[arg(long)] job: String }` and the handler function to take `StatusArgs { job: String, endpoint: Url }`. Post-step: scenario §1.1 is the walking-skeleton acceptance gate for the whole feature; this step's GREEN is the feature's GREEN. cargo mutants kill rate ≥80% on `commands::alloc::status` handler body + any CLI-side digest-compute path per testing.md mandatory target 'hash determinism paths'.

scenarios: §1.1 test-scenarios.md (WS-1) — Ana submits a job and reads a byte-identical spec digest back from overdrive alloc status
§1.2 test-scenarios.md (WS-2) — overdrive cluster status shows the noop-heartbeat reconciler registered with broker counters (turned GREEN in step 05-03, re-asserted by §1.1 composition in 05-05)
§1.3 test-scenarios.md (WS-3) — byte-identical resubmit is idempotent; different spec at same key is a conflict (turned GREEN by 03-04 server-side; CLI-side surface integrated at 05-05)
walking_skeleton.strategy: C (real local) per DWD-01 — every @walking_skeleton scenario uses real rcgen CA, real axum + rustls, real redb LocalStore, real libsql, real reqwest. Per `crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md` (firm rule), the CLI is exercised by direct library-handler calls (not by spawning `overdrive` as a subprocess) — the `overdrive_cli::commands::{cluster, serve, job, alloc}` handlers are invoked in-process on a single tokio runtime with the server on a sibling task. The integration shape remains Strategy C (no mocks on infrastructure); only the CLI-to-server coupling is in-process rather than inter-process. Deleting any of the real infrastructure adapters breaks the WS — the litmus test in DWD-01 has been verified.
walking_skeleton.definition_of_done: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests -E 'test(walking_skeleton)'` is GREEN on a clean clone after `cargo xtask openapi-gen` has been run once. Specifically (all via direct `overdrive_cli::commands` handler invocation, no subprocess): `cluster::init(...)` writes a trust triple at the target config dir; `serve::run(...)` binds TLS on an ephemeral 127.0.0.1 port and returns a `ServeHandle`; `job::submit(...)` returns `Ok(SubmitOutput { commit_index >= 1, ... })`; `alloc::status(...)` returns `Ok(AllocStatusOutput)` whose `spec_digest` is byte-identical to the local rkyv compute and whose `empty_state_message` contains `phase-1-first-workload`; `serve_handle.shutdown().await` cleanly drains in-flight requests.

tdd_rhythm_per_step: 1. RED (acceptance): translate the DISTILL scenario(s) cited in `criteria` into a failing Rust #[test] / #[tokio::test] in the step's test_file; confirm it fails for a business-logic reason (not a compile error from a missing scaffold — the scaffold panics are the RED baseline).

2. RED (unit, optional): if the GREEN minimal implementation requires internal decomposition, add failing unit tests that drive each extracted helper. Skip if the acceptance test drives GREEN on its own.
3. GREEN: minimal implementation replacing the specific `SCAFFOLD: true` markers named in the step's notes. Do NOT add fields or methods not required by an active test.

4. REFACTOR: apply .claude/rules/development.md (Result alias per CLAUDE.md, pass-through #[from], newtype completeness, rkyv canonical hashing, no Instant::now in core, reconciler I/O contract, &str over String in internal APIs, parking_lot over std::sync where appropriate). Apply Object Calisthenics in the hexagonal core per quality-framework.

5. PROPTEST: where the step's scope covers a testing.md mandatory call site (newtype roundtrip, rkyv roundtrip, snapshot roundtrip, hash determinism, LWW convergence, intent-key derivation), add the proptest block with PROPTEST_CASES >= 1024 (default). Steps tagged `@property` in test-scenarios.md MUST land a proptest, not a single-example assertion.

6. MUTATION: run `cargo xtask mutants --in-diff origin/main --package <affected_crate>` locally; kill rate >=80% on the diff per testing.md mandatory target list. Unjustified surviving mutants block the COMMIT; justified skips carry `// mutants: skip` + a comment per testing.md §Rules.

7. POST-GREEN WIRING CHECK: `git diff --name-only` shows every file in implementation_scope.production_files actually changed. If only test files changed but tests flipped RED→GREEN, it's Fixture Theater — BLOCK the commit, return to GREEN. Per quality-framework Pattern 8.
8. SCAFFOLD INVENTORY: `rg 'SCAFFOLD: true' crates/ xtask/` — confirm only intentional scaffolds remain (some survive across steps until the owning slice closes; the step's notes declare which).

9. COMMIT: one commit per step; message footer carries the step id (e.g. `step: 03-01`); `--no-verify` is sanctioned while other scaffolds are RED but the commit must be `--verify`-clean on files IT touched (scoped `cargo fmt` + `cargo clippy` + `cargo nextest run -p <crate>`).
